title: buildah-run: fix out-of-range panic
body:
Fix an out-of-range panic in buildah-run by moving the call to
setNonbloc() to the appropriate place (i.e., only when the copyPipes
parameter is set).

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <rothberg@redhat.com>

comments.body: @giuseppe PTAL
LGTM, but someone more familiar with the code should have a look
@vrothberg Did you actually hit this?  Should we stop the release of buildah 1.8.4 and move to 1.8.5?

Multiple hits, I -1'd both 1.8.4 kits.

Testing looks good so far, if it continues we should spin 1.9.0 with this in it.
Ok any chance this leaks into podman build?
@rh-atomic-bot r+

:pushpin: Commit c14d21c has been approved by `rhatdan`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=rhatdan c14d21c75696b2abd8467015eaa917363ea7c96d -->
:hourglass: Testing commit c14d21c with merge 4821392...
I don't think it spills into the build, that looked OK, but if we can squeeze this into podman, I'd feel more comfortable about it.  @mheon, WDYT?
I'm waiting on one more PR already, but it's getting late in the day over here
@TomSweeneyRedHat Could you begin preparing a release for 1.9.0.

Dang it, travis failed.
ack on 1.9.0 @rhatdatn

Hanging here in two of the three test streams:
```
Bad: 

ok 295 rmi image that is created from another named image
No output has been received in the last 10m0s, this potentially indicates a stalled build or something wrong with the build itself.
Check the details on how to adjust your build configuration on: https://docs.travis-ci.com/user/common-build-problems/#Build-times-out-because-no-output-was-received
```

```
Good:

ok 295 rmi image that is created from another named image
ok 296 run
ok 297 run--args
```

Which I think @giuseppe was fighting with in the original PR.

> @vrothberg Did you actually hit this? Should we stop the release of buildah 1.8.4 and move to 1.8.5?

Yes, I can reproduce with a `$ buildah run $CTR ls`.
FWIW, Podman looks OK from my limited testing.  Looks to be a 'buildah run' only issue.
Thanks for the ping, @TomSweeneyRedHat! I updated the PR now. It seems that the reading fds need to be set to non-blocking before Stdin.
This LGTM and passes baseline tests.  I think @vrothberg has solved the test issue too.

@vrothberg  no good deed goes unpunished.

```
# Run gofmt on version 1.11 and higher
run_linux.go:1064::warning: cyclomatic complexity 46 of function runCopyStdio() is high (> 45) (gocyclo)
Makefile:66: recipe for target 'validate' failed
make: *** [validate] Error 1
```
Replaced by #1674

> FWIW, Podman looks OK from my limited testing. Looks to be a 'buildah run' only issue.

Issue happens on newest versions of Podman 1.4.1 with Buildah 1.8.4

**Error output:**

```
STEP 5: RUN chmod -R g=u /opt/{ansible,apb}
panic: runtime error: index out of range

goroutine 19 [running]:
github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.runCopyStdio(0xc0008271d0, 0x2b00, 0xc0000c5900, 0x3, 0x3, 0x1, 0xc00083fb30, 0xc0008271e0, 0x2, 0x2, ...)
        /build/podman/src/src/github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/run_linux.go:1137 +0x2502
created by github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah.runUsingRuntime
        /build/podman/src/src/github.com/containers/libpod/vendor/github.com/containers/buildah/run_linux.go:769 +0x10b9
Error: error building at STEP "RUN chmod -R g=u /opt/{ansible,apb}": error while running runtime: exit status 2
```
FYI @mheon
@SamyCoenen can you share your Dockerfile by chance?

@TomSweeneyRedHat Sure

LABEL "com.redhat.apb.spec"=\

bjogVGhpcyBkZWZhdWx0IHBsYW4gZGVwbG95cyBjdF9rOHNfYXBwCiAgICBmcmVlOiBUcnVlCiAg\
ICBtZXRhZGF0YToge30KICAgIHBhcmFtZXRlcnM6IFtd"

COPY playbooks /opt/apb/actions

USER apb

@SamyCoenen Perfect!  TYVM!
